text.title: django-pytest-parametrize Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-pytest-parametrize as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Parameter lists so long they become unmaintainable (30+ entries). The test body is lost in the data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Split large parameter lists into logical groups with separate parametrized functions (test_valid_emails, test_invalid_emails). Each function tests one category of behavior. This keeps the test intent clear and failures easy to understand.

failure-mode.description: Parametrizing over database objects (model instances) directly in the parameter list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model instances cannot exist outside a database transaction. Parametrize over IDs, strings, or dicts and resolve the objects inside the test function using fixtures. Direct model references in parameter lists cause database errors at collection time.

failure-mode.description: Using generic ids like test_validation[0], test_validation[1] without meaningful names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When test_validation[3] fails at 2am in CI, there is no context. Use descriptive tuple comments, the ids= parameter, or pytest.param(value, id="name") to give each case a meaningful name.
